questions.question: Which of the following viral diseases causes devastating economic losses in poultry farming?
Which of the following is NOT an advantage of Artificial Insemination?

options.A: It ensures a 100% success rate in conception in all cases
options.B: It allows the semen of one superior male to impregnate multiple females
options.C: It helps in overcoming problems of normal mating
options.D: Semen can be frozen and transported over long distances

questions.question: What is the primary reason why interspecific hybrids like Mules are generally not used for further breeding programs?

options.A: They are typically sterile due to chromosomal mismatch during meiosis
options.B: They lack resistance to common equine diseases
options.C: They undergo severe inbreeding depression immediately
options.D: Their lifespan is too short to reach sexual maturity

questions.question: In MOET process, a super-ovulating cow will produce approximately how many eggs per cycle instead of the normal one egg?
In bee hives, what determines the caste of a female bee (whether she becomes a Queen or a Worker)?

options.A: The diet fed during the larval stage (Royal Jelly vs. Bee Bread)
options.C: The temperature of the incubation cell
options.D: The genetic make-up inherited only from the drone

questions.question: In the context of fisheries, what role did the 'Blue Revolution' play?

options.A: It led to the modernization and massive increase in fish and aquatic production
options.B: It successfully introduced genetically modified marine algae into the ocean
options.C: It replaced all freshwater aquaculture with marine pisciculture
options.D: It caused the eradication of parasitic infections in all commercially farmed fish

questions.question: Which major bacterial disease affects cattle, sheep, and goats, often leading to sudden death and posing a severe zoonotic risk to humans handling the wool?

The success rate of crossing mature male and female cattle can be relatively low even with artificial insemination. Which technology is frequently coupled with AI to improve herd improvement rates drastically?
Which of the following is NOT an objective of cross-breeding in animal husbandry?

options.B: Combining the desirable qualities of two different breeds
options.C: Introducing new genetic variations into a herd

questions.question: Interspecific hybridization often produces offspring that are robust but sterile. Which of the following is the most classic example?
When developing the Hisardale sheep breed, what was the primary breeding method used?
